creator: dinoex@FreeBSD.org
text:
I can not reproduce your problem yet.

I still use afterstep as my main window manager.
10.2-RELEASE amd64

# ldd /usr/local/bin/afterstep 
/usr/local/bin/afterstep:
        libXpm.so.4 => /usr/local/lib/libXpm.so.4 (0x800847000)
        libXext.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXext.so.6 (0x800a5a000)
        libX11.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libX11.so.6 (0x800c6b000)
        libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x800fa4000)
        libintl.so.8 => /usr/local/lib/libintl.so.8 (0x801350000)
        libxcb.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libxcb.so.1 (0x80155b000)
        librpcsvc.so.5 => /usr/lib/librpcsvc.so.5 (0x80177a000)
        libXau.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXau.so.6 (0x801983000)
        libpthread-stubs.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libpthread-stubs.so.0 (0x801b85000)
        libXdmcp.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXdmcp.so.6 (0x801d86000)


are the dependecies you use up to date?

is it something in your .steprc or in your X11 config?
Please check and report.
